strings:
The spirit of Corak proclaims,
"Fantastic adventure awaits you.
Challenging quests abound and battles
innumerable must be fought. Do you
have the might to emerge victorious?
Only time will tell..."

The humble wizard Nordon asks, "Will
you do me a service (y/n)?"

"Numerous rewards will be yours if you
retreive one of my magical golden
goblets, ruthlessly stolen by the
dreaded goblins who dwell in the cave
below!"

"Ah, you've found a goblet! For your
bravery I grant you 2,000 exp, the
spell Eagle Eye, and if you search,
1,000 gold."

"Now, visit my sister Nordonna. She
lives nearby and could provide
valuable information."
"Remember, you must find my goblet."
"Remember, you must visit my sister."
"Such a pity. Now kindly begone!"

Nordonna moans, "My kidnapped sons are
held captive by the cruel kobolds in
the cave under this town. You've
braved this treacherous place for my
brother, now rescue Drog and Sir Hyron
and I'll reward you well!"
Nordonna has gone fishing.
Nordonna yelps, "Begone strangers!"
"Please, rescue my children soon."

"My grateful sons are now available
for hire at Middlegate Inn. I reward
you with information to help you gain
untold riches! Travel through portals
to all towns, donate at the temples,
then visit Feldecarb Fountain."

A sleepy conjurer yawns, "My friends,
for only 20 gold I can enroll you in
the local mage guild." Buy in (y/n)?
Not enough gold!

Fanciful Feldecarb Fountain flows
full as fluttering faeries frolic
fastidiously. Flick a farthing (y/n)?
You find a fabulous castle key!
Fool, you have no farthing to flick!

The mystic green glow of an ancient
key catches your eye. The locksmith
offers it for 500 gold. Pay (y/n)?

The spirit of Corak proclaims,
"Fantastic adventure awaits you.
Challenging quests abound and battles
innumerable must be fought. Do you
have the might to emerge victorious?
Only time will tell..."

13.text:

strings:
A uniformed brownie stands before a
shimmering energy field offering
instaneous travel to Sandsobar for
10 gold. Travel (y/n)?
Not enough gold!

A bored magician languidly offers
enrollment into the local mage guild
for 50,000 gold. Pay (y/n)?

The teary-eyed locksmith groans, "For
you, I will part with my beloved jet
Black Key for 50,000 gold." Pay (y/n)?
*** Backpacks Full ***

The Dread Knight resides in B3-5,14.
A knight must defeat him. Many have
tried, all have failed.

Baron Wilfrey's lair is in B2-11,2.
It is  rumored that only a mighty
archer may defeat him.

Brutal Bruno, the Barbarian Chieftan,
asserts his authority over all
barbarians in C4-0,15.

The vixen Dawn, rumored to have more
lives than a Cat from Hell, hides from
ninja assassins in her Mist Bog
in D4-8,9.

The evil wizard Ybmug left 23 right 46
The good wizard Yekop left 64 right 32
Interested sorcerers should inquire at
the two castles, Isle of the Ancients.

Find Corak's soul in area C1-10,15.
Clerics should reunite it with his
heavily guarded body.

Paladins should rid
the Forbidden Forest of its dungeon
dwelling Frost Dragon at 8,8.

Sly robbers should accompany at least
one class or maybe all. They slink
unnoticed, yet reap many rewards.
Learn more from the Jurors of
Mt. Farview in D2-7,0.

A harrassed cleric and a battered
archer look up at you in surprise. The
cleric says, "I am Big Bootay and this
is Cleogotcha. Thank you for freeing
us. We were imprisoned falsely for
uncouth behavior by (space)

the stodgy Atlantians. We are now
available for hire at the
Carriage Inn."

Falsely accused, you serve two years
of a life sentence and are released
with a sincere apology.

the stodgy Atlantians. We are now

strings:
Tired of getting lost? For just 750
gold, Chris will teach you how to use
a sextant properly. Learn (y/n)?
Who will learn this skill (1-8)?
Not enough gold!
You already have two skills!

Gertrude and Rat Fink thank you for
freeing them from the Snowbeast's
pantry. They are now available for
hire at the Tundaran Arms Inn.

Strewn about the lair are body parts
and gnawed bones. By a half-eaten
carcass a bejeweled hand wears an
Emerald Ring. Rob the dead (y/n)?
*** Backpacks Full ***

Town guards grab you. "Aha, you have
the Emerald Ring. You have kidnapped
the old woman! Go directly to jail!"

In the gutter lies a worn copy of the
Tundaran Chronicle reading, "Strange
dissappearances and mutilations of
innocent residents continue!"

Beware of the Snowbeast or you will
end up like me!

"I am Sgt. Stephen Pain. For 500 gold
join my private hell and increase your
pathetic stamina." Train (y/n)?

The enormous gladiator Spartacus will
mercilessly beat you for 500 gold. You
will be stronger. Pay the brute (y/n)?

Learn the ancient secrets every weapon
hides. The master of accuracy charges
500 gold. Do you pay (y/n)?
Water, Fire, Earth, and Air
All have a King in their Lair
They hold the talons you need to find
To save Cron before its time

A Barbarian and a Sorcerer confront
you. "I'm Thund R. and this is Aeriel.
We like your style and you may hire us
any time. Look us up at Hotel Four.
We'll be waiting."

While deftly shaping the curves of a
slender Yellow Key, the shopkeeper
announces, "Finished at last! Buy this
little beauty for 1000 gold (y/n)?"

Sallow-eyed beggars and cripples crowd
the alley pleading for a handout. Toss
them a gold piece (y/n)?
Day 93 is Nature's day.

A horde of Giant Rats gnaw on a still
struggling victim. Help (y/n)?

strings:
H.K. Phooey approaches, "I like you,
you have good taste in food. Look for
me at the Hourglass Inn."

Rinaldo, the ultimate ambassador, will
instruct you in the gentle art of
diplomacy for 500 gold. Learn (y/n)?
Who will learn this skill (1-8)?
You already have two skills!
Not enough gold!

Sly, a seedy looking rogue, will teach
you how to pickpocket for 300 gold.
Pay (y/n)?

Lucky Spade, the best gambler alive,
will teach you his system for 200
gold. Take this special course (y/n)?

A shivering wizard sells lifetime
membership for the Tundaran Mage
Guild for the low, low price of 2000
gold. Pay (y/n)?

A one-eyed beggar will teach your
party the Wizard Eye spell for 100
gold. Pay (y/n)?

Sorry, I will only train Ninjas and
Robbers!

As chairs and tables fly, a mug hits
you in the face. Brawl (y/n)?

Lucindra hints, "New tips, on odd,
even, every 30th, and 180th day."

strings: B

Lord Haart wonders if you could do
his house a favor (y/n)?
He leaves you with a sigh of disgust.

Two of my ancestors held items of 
legend which brought great honor to
the House of Haart. In the seventh
century, Spaz Twit owned a phaser. In
the eighth century, a man known simply
as The Long One owned a ceremonial...

loincloth. Return these items to me
and you shall be rewarded.
I
T

By a pit of brown gunk, a nubian
eunich announces, "We use only the
finest mud mixed with volcanic ash
imported from distant Vulcania. Take
a mud bath (y/n)?"

Two scantily clad Atlantian maidens
bearing baskets of rose petals beckon
you to the waters of the hot springs.
Bathe (y/n)?
You feel rejuvenated.

Paying guests ONLY!
See the travel agent.

An old druid cackles, "Your breath
really reeks of my favorite, Red Hot
Wolf Nipple Chips! Friends, take this
spell, Nature's Gate."

Scholars and sages flow in and out of
mystic Castle Xabran. Enter (y/n)?

Castle Guards exclaim, "No key,
no admittance!" Attack (y/n)?

Eerie shadows dance off the six
megalithic stones that stand ominously
in a semicircle. From a bloodstained
sacrificial altar, a voice booms,
"Password?"

The altar moves, revealing an ancient
well opening. Go down (y/n)?
"Wrong! Look to the stones."

Lord Haart wonders if you could do
his house a favor (y/n)?

10.text: He leaves you with a sigh of disgust.

11.text:
Two of my ancestors held items of 
legend which brought great honor to
the House of Haart. In the seventh
century, Spaz Twit owned a phaser. In
the eighth century, a man known simply
as The Long One owned a ceremonial...

12.text:
loincloth. Return these items to me
and you shall be rewarded.

15.text:
By a pit of brown gunk, a nubian
eunich announces, "We use only the
finest mud mixed with volcanic ash
imported from distant Vulcania. Take
a mud bath (y/n)?"

16.text:
Two scantily clad Atlantian maidens
bearing baskets of rose petals beckon
you to the waters of the hot springs.
Bathe (y/n)?

18.text:
Paying guests ONLY!
See the travel agent.

19.text:

really reeks of my favorite, Red Hot
Wolf Nipple Chips! Friends, take this
spell, Nature's Gate."

strings:
Wait 'til Peabody hears about this!"
says Sherman, while crafty Nakazawa
bows in thanks. "Thanks for saving us
from those crazy natives. If you want,
look for us at the Carriage Inn."

Nestled in the open-air pavillion
rests the Gourmet. He wishes to speak
with a true epicurean. Will you
talk with him (y/n)?
Who will talk with the Gourmet (1-8)?

Ah, I appreciate a fellow connoisseur.
You are truly an epicurean
(+100,000 experience).

I speak only to epicureans, especially
those who really enjoy a good meal.

You stare at the bizarre people in
front of you. Fumbler is covered with
lucky symbols and you cannot tell if
Flailer is a guy or a girl! Never-
the-less, they can be found at the
Carriage Inn for hire.

strings: Y
Y

You reunite Corak's soul with his en-
tombed body, and he arises before you.

"Thank you for freeing me. Go to Mount
Farview to gain your reward. Also, I
have advice for you. All encasement
spells are one square away and one
square over from the most distant
corner of each elemental plane."

Strewn across an ornate throne upon an
ivory pedestal rests Murray, once
among mightiest of warriors and now
most wealthy of all in Cron. This
proud and powerful man has a troubled
look on his brow. Help him (y/n)?

Murray's foul adversary, Dawn, the
wench from Hell, has corrupted his
Isle. Foul things happen at his resort
instead of pleasurable. This condition
must end. He dismisses you with a
simple request-"Kill Dawn and return."
Murray is on vacation. Try again soon.
Kill Dawn NOW!

My undying thanks! The wench from Hell
is really dead? Wonderful! You must
search for your reward! Goodbye!
Remember, the Horvath must die!

"You have rid the world of a great
evil. In recompense, here is the
sacred spell, Divine Intervention.
Use it wisely."

The Elder Druid acknowledges your
party and wonders if you would perform
a service for him in exchange for a
great reward (y/n)?
"Then leave my abode!"

"A disciple of mine sought power out
on his own. He made one mistake too
many and now he must be exterminated!
Find him in this cave and return to me
when you have finished him."

Knee-high in putrid water, you notice
human and animal bones, skulls and
carved votive figures lodged in mud.

A box on the wall reads, "Break glass
if in need of Hit Points."
Shatter glass (y/n)?

All who are needy receive 1000
Hit Points.

You reunite Corak's soul with his en-
tombed body, and he arises before you.

10.text:
"Thank you for freeing me. Go to Mount
Farview to gain your reward. Also, I
have advice for you. All encasement
spells are one square away and one
square over from the most distant
corner of each elemental plane."

11.text:
Strewn across an ornate throne upon an
ivory pedestal rests Murray, once
among mightiest of warriors and now
most wealthy of all in Cron. This
proud and powerful man has a troubled
look on his brow. Help him (y/n)?

12.text:
Murray's foul adversary, Dawn, the
wench from Hell, has corrupted his
Isle. Foul things happen at his resort
instead of pleasurable. This condition
must end. He dismisses you with a
simple request-"Kill Dawn and return."

13.text: Murray is on vacation. Try again soon.

15.text:
My undying thanks! The wench from Hell
is really dead? Wonderful! You must
search for your reward! Goodbye!

17.text:
"You have rid the world of a great
evil. In recompense, here is the
sacred spell, Divine Intervention.
Use it wisely."

18.text:
The Elder Druid acknowledges your
party and wonders if you would perform
a service for him in exchange for a
great reward (y/n)?

20.text:
"A disciple of mine sought power out
on his own. He made one mistake too
many and now he must be exterminated!
Find him in this cave and return to me
when you have finished him."

21.text:

human and animal bones, skulls and
carved votive figures lodged in mud.

if in need of Hit Points."
Shatter glass (y/n)?

Hit Points.

strings: You've got personality!
ZOT!

The bloody cleric, Holy Moley, and his
sidekick, the shifty Slick Pick, pass
out before you. Target practice can be
brutal! Hire at the Hourglass Inn.

A bedraggled cleric and a battered
robber are being used as targets by a
group of monsters. Intercede (y/n)?

The sword of courage, shining symbol
of the staunch of heart, remains
unclaimed in D4 at 14,11.

To find the true angle on the Evil
Wizard Ybmug follow the Dark Path:
1-3-1-(3 or 7)-9-11-A-C-G-I.

To find the correct approach to the
Good Wizard Yekop, follow the path of
light:2-2-4-6-6-12-A-D-F-I.

If time enough is spent in the
Elemental Plane of Air, square 1,14
should be seen for Encasement
purposes.

The Elder Druid of Druid's Cave has in
his hands the secrets needed to cast
Divine Intervention.

Holy Word is best found facing south
while walking through the woods.

You wrest the Orb from its
pedestal and take it for your own.

Travel to and around the Elemental
Plane of Water where Encasement can be
found at 1,1.

Harry Kari and No Name can be found,
simply sitting around, in the Cavern
under Vulcania, at 1,14.

Flailer and Fumbler have been taken
captive and chained among many others
at the Pearl Islands.

Posing as monster-attractive targets,
Holy Moley and Slick Pick are
entertaining monsters at Dawn's Mist
Bog Cavern.
You find several gold items.
*** Backpacks Full ***

Etched in mud, a message on the floor
reads: "Look in Castle Xabran at 6,2
and you will find the Earth Disc."

Find Red Duke and Dead Eye held
captive in Ambush Valley at 14,1.

The Element Orb waits at 10,15 in
Dawn's Mist Bog Cavern.

Hastily written by a shaky hand, a
message reads, "Fluxer, Radicon,
Todilor and Capitor are essential to
gain the Element Orb."

strings:
"Knights must defeat the Dread Knight
of Jouster's Way. Paladins must defeat
the Frost Dragon who resides in the
dungeon beneath the Forbidden Forest.
Archers must stop Baron Wilfrey from
terrorizing Falcon Forest."

"Clerics have to reunite the soul of
mighty Corak with his body. Sorcerers
must release both the Good and the
Evil Wizards from the Isle of the
Ancients. Ninjas must assassinate the
chaotic Dawn of Dawn's Mist..."

"...Bog. Barbarians must defeat Brutal
Bruno, the Barbarian Chieftain."

strings:
Weary Queen Lamanda perks up,"At last,
the true and brave triple crown win-
ners! From your party, I bequeath a
Chosen One to save my father and our
beloved Cron. See Lord Peabody to
change history.

Distraught Queen Lamanda wails,"Whilst
my courtiers are merry, a heavy burden
I must carry. If only you gained your
Plus and won the Triple Crown.
"Remember to rescue my father!"

King Kalohn sits once again upon his
throne, proclaiming,"Now you face your
gravest task of all. Go to Square Lake
with the password WAFE. The future of
CRON is at stake!"

"Remember to solve the mystery at
Square Lake!"

Trumpets sound and courtiers cheer.
King Kalohn exclaims,"Saviors of CRON,
you are always welcome here."

Gwyndon, Corak's apprentice, is busily
packing away his belongings before he
goes to Lord Peabody's. He says that
Corak's body is hidden in the cave in
Corak's Woods and that Corak's Soul is
at 10,15 in the Lost Soul's Woods.

"Hi, how have you been? It's marvelous
to see you again!"

Lord Peabody needs the help of
a Crusader.

"Please return to me with my boy,
Sherman, and I will reward you."

Lord Peabody needs some help.
Will you offer your services (y/n)?
"Don't come back without Sherman!"

"Thank you very, very much. You have
done well. As a reward, you may use
the time machine in the corner."

19.text:
Weary Queen Lamanda perks up,"At last,
the true and brave triple crown win-
ners! From your party, I bequeath a
Chosen One to save my father and our
beloved Cron. See Lord Peabody to
change history.

20.text:
Distraught Queen Lamanda wails,"Whilst
my courtiers are merry, a heavy burden
I must carry. If only you gained your
Plus and won the Triple Crown.

22.text:
King Kalohn sits once again upon his
throne, proclaiming,"Now you face your
gravest task of all. Go to Square Lake
with the password WAFE. The future of
CRON is at stake!"

23.text:
"Remember to solve the mystery at
Square Lake!"

24.text:
Trumpets sound and courtiers cheer.
King Kalohn exclaims,"Saviors of CRON,
you are always welcome here."

25.text:
Gwyndon, Corak's apprentice, is busily
packing away his belongings before he
goes to Lord Peabody's. He says that
Corak's body is hidden in the cave in
Corak's Woods and that Corak's Soul is
at 10,15 in the Lost Soul's Woods.

26.text:
"Hi, how have you been? It's marvelous
to see you again!"

28.text:
Lord Peabody needs the help of
a Crusader.

29.text:
"Please return to me with my boy,
Sherman, and I will reward you."

30.text:
Lord Peabody needs some help.
Will you offer your services (y/n)?

32.text:
"Thank you very, very much. You have
done well. As a reward, you may use
the time machine in the corner."

strings:
Welcome to the Hall of Spells! We have
a list of spells which can only be
found. They list as follows: C-Cleric
or S-Sorcerer, level/spell #, area
found in + coordinates. Happy Hunting!

Welcome to Hireling Hall! We have a
list of hard to find hirelings.
They are listed by power (weakest to
strongest, and are all pairs except 2
and 13), area found in, coordinates +
town they hire out of. Happy Hiring!

Yellow Interleave - every two letters
should be matched 'til the end. Try
this way: 9-5-6-1-7-4-2-8-3.

The Bishop of Green Battle is locked
in a cage.

The Bishop of Green Battle is freed
from imprisonment by your green key.
(+3000 exp). He grants all green
triple crown winners 10,000 exp.

The two prisoners flee in a puff of
magical smoke. A note remains. It says
that Lord Slayer chained them because
they killed an Earth Wyrm by magic
instead of by might. Reach them at
the Hourglass Inn.

A wizard appears and says, "Come
close, I give you deal. I'm a little
down on my luck, so I'm willing to let
you have THIS for just 2 million
gold." He pulls out an old, rotting
book. It's titled:

"How to Succeed in Spell-Casting
Without Really Trying." Buy it (y/n)?

Hey! What are you trying to pull.
Make sure that the first member of
your party has the cash or no deal!

You open the book and it vanishes with
a flash. You have learned all spells!
You spot the wizard cornering another
party,"I'm a little down on my luck.."

Castle guards peddle Uncle Spudly's
New and Improved Quest Removal Elixir.
It can be yours to drink for only
19 gold. Buy it (y/n)?
Not enough gold!

This stuff works great! Your
quest has been removed.
